summary: Matrix recording with occasional crowd‑noise oddities and mid‑set echo, yet an outstanding 1977 show highlighted by a spectacular Eyes of the World.
review: The performance is described as simply amazing – every song works and the 1977 vibe shines through, especially on the transcendent Eyes of the World. The matrix source adds atmosphere despite some strange crowd‑noise cuts and an echo issue halfway through Ship of Fools, but overall it’s considered superior to the Betty board version.

ai_rating.rationale: A single enthusiastic 5‑star review praises both the musical performance and the matrix recording, noting only minor technical quirks; thus a high rating with low confidence due to limited data.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crowd‑noise between songs sounds odd (possible tape cuts) and there is noticeable echo on vocals/instruments around halfway through Ship of Fools, but the overall mix is clear and adds to the experience.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Consistently strong throughout with no bad songs; the set maintains high energy and cohesion typical of a great 1977 show.
